title: Ethical Guidelines for Peer Reviewers
notes: Core duties for competence, conflicts, confidentiality, timeliness, conduct, and bounded requests

title: Peer review core practice and resources
notes: Use with the detailed ethical guideline and current target-journal policy

title: Responsibilities in the Submission and Peer-Review Process
notes: Confidentiality, deletion, assistance disclosure, conflicts, constructive review, and editor responsibility

title: Use of AI by Reviewers
notes: Follow journal policy, request permission, preserve confidentiality, disclose use, and validate output

title: Library for health research reporting
notes: Primary registry for selecting study-type-specific health reporting guidance

title: Selecting the appropriate reporting guideline for your article
notes: Selection aid for authors editors and peer reviewers

title: CONSORT 2025 statement: updated guideline for reporting randomised trials
notes: Thirty main items; supersedes 2010; explicitly not a quality assessment instrument

title: SPIRIT 2025 statement: updated guideline for protocols of randomised trials
notes: Thirty-four main items; supersedes SPIRIT 2013

title: PRISMA 2020
notes: Statement, expanded checklist, abstract checklist, and flow diagrams

title: The PRISMA 2020 statement: an updated guideline for reporting systematic reviews
notes: Explicitly not intended to assess conduct or methodological quality

title: Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology
notes: Use design-specific checklist and applicable extensions

title: STARD 2015: updated list for diagnostic accuracy studies
notes: Thirty main reporting items for diagnostic accuracy studies

title: STARD reporting guideline applicability guidance
notes: Explicitly separates reporting assessment from design-quality appraisal

title: The STARD-AI reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence
notes: Forty items for AI-centered diagnostic accuracy studies

title: TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for clinical prediction models
notes: Twenty-seven items; replaces TRIPOD 2015 for regression and machine-learning prediction models

title: CARE Checklist
notes: Case reports including patient perspective and informed consent

title: The ARRIVE Guidelines 2.0
notes: Essential 10 plus Recommended Set for in vivo animal research

title: SQUIRE 2.0 Guidelines
notes: Quality-improvement reporting; not every element applies to every manuscript

title: CHEERS 2022
notes: Replaces 2013 guidance for health economic evaluations

title: SAMPL guidelines for statistical reporting
notes: Succinct biomedical statistical reporting guidance for authors editors and reviewers

title: Statement on Statistical Significance and P-Values
notes: Do not base conclusions on thresholds alone; report transparently and distinguish effect size from significance

title: Guidance: Rigor and Reproducibility in Grant Applications
notes: Rigor of prior research, robust design, authentication, and transparent reporting

title: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands and Sensitivity Analysis in Clinical Trials
notes: Trial estimands missing data and sensitivity analysis; use only when applicable

title: MIAME and MINSEQE guidelines
notes: GEO still implements both standards; verify current repository submission fields

title: MIAPE reporting guidelines for proteomics
notes: Modular released components have separate versions; select the component matching the workflow

organization: International Society for Advancement of Cytometry
title: MIFlowCyt
notes: Retained as active ISAC guidance for flow-cytometry experiment reporting

title: MIAPPE Releases
notes: Version 2.0 remained in early development at verification date

title: Minimum Information about any Sequence
notes: MIGS and MIMS are legacy checklists within the current MIxS umbrella

title: Peer Review
notes: Example only; prohibits uploading manuscripts to generative AI and requests AI-use declaration

title: AI use
notes: Example only; protects unpublished material and requires declaration for review-language assistance

title: Guidance for Authors Peer Reviewers and Editors on Use of AI Language Models and Chatbots
notes: Example only; prohibits entering manuscript or review text into a chatbot and requires disclosure
